decision_table.opaque_append_works_with_fixed_fp16_owne: NO -- microbench passes but model-local bakes from step 1
decision_table.remaining_blocker_model_route_or_core_li: CORE TinyJit/HCQ LIFECYCLE -- the materialization IS the @function cross-replay persistence; removing it (opaque append) loses persistence. NOT a model-route bug, NOT the tile, NOT arg-patching.
decision_table.expected_wd_if_solved: >=5% (the MAXC-shrink shows +11.8%; at MAXC=1280 tinygrad reaches ~97 tok/s = ~llama parity)

rationale: MAXC-shrink PROVES the materialization is on the critical path and worth ~+11.8% (would reach llama parity). But the copy-free opaque append fails at the full-model graph/persistence layer EVEN with the fixed fp16 owned tile -- the failure is the core runtime lifecycle coupling (materialization == @function persistence), not a bounded model-route primitive. Per the scope decision rule: MAXC transfers + opaque append fails at graph/persistence => CORE_RUNTIME_BLOCKED. Do NOT attempt a broad TinyJit/HCQ persistence redesign in this task.
